task_completion.description: The grayscale landscape image is opened in an image editing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A grayscale landscape photograph is visible within the workspace of an image editor (such as GIMP).

task_completion.description: The Threshold tool (or equivalent high-contrast conversion tool) is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Threshold' dialog box or panel is visible on the screen, typically accessed via a 'Colors' menu.

task_completion.description: The threshold settings are adjusted, showing a high-contrast preview on the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the canvas changes to a stark black and white preview while the Threshold dialog is still open and active.

task_completion.description: The threshold conversion is applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Threshold dialog is closed or confirmed, and the main image canvas displays the updated black and white image.

task_completion.description: The final image successfully exhibits a high-contrast black and white effect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final image consists predominantly of pure black and pure white pixels (binary dominance), with smooth grayscale gradients eliminated.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the conversion.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with application menus (e.g., Colors -> Threshold) and dialog boxes, rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The resulting image is a genuine modification of the original input.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final black and white image retains the exact same landscape composition as the original grayscale image, proving it wasn't swapped with a pre-existing file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors or IDEs open with Python/shell scripts used to process the image in the background.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses real landscape photographs from Unsplash. The expected method is using GIMP's Threshold tool (accessible via Colors -> Threshold). A successful conversion will result in an image with binary dominance (predominantly pure black and pure white pixels, ≥80%) and meaningful modification from the original grayscale image, eliminating smooth grayscale gradients.
